stories.title: I-036 systemctl poweroff PolicyKit fix -- eclipse-obd can shutdown
stories.intent: PLACEHOLDER. Fix the PolicyKit auth fail that prevents eclipse-obd.service (running as User=mcornelison) from invoking systemctl poweroff at TRIGGER. Latent since V0.24.1 -- all drains 10-22 likely hard-crashed at buck-dropout floor (~3.30V) instead of graceful shutdown. Spool recommend Option A: polkit rule at /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/50-eclipse-obd-poweroff.rules allowing mcornelison user + org.freedesktop.login1.power-off action -> polkit.Result.YES. Alternates: B (AmbientCapabilities=CAP_SYS_BOOT) or C (sudoers NOPASSWD). Final option choice owned by CIO+Ralph.

filesToTouch: deploy/polkit-rules/50-eclipse-obd-poweroff.rules (NEW PLACEHOLDER -- polkit rule for Option A; alternate paths under Option B/C; CIO+Ralph finalize)
src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- _executeShutdown error-handling + structured logging on PolicyKit denial)
tests/pi/power/test_orchestrator_poweroff_auth.py (NEW PLACEHOLDER -- synthetic regression: mock subprocess call to systemctl poweroff; assert handler interprets exit-1+stderr correctly)
doNotTouch: battery_health_log close-out path (writes runtime_seconds + end_timestamp BEFORE shutdown; preserve order)
V0.24.1 ladder stage WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER logic (orthogonal -- ladder fires correctly, only terminal poweroff fails)

groundingRefs.value: Drain 22 journal: 22:53:09 _executeShutdown Shutdown command returned non-zero: 1. stderr: Call to PowerOff failed: Interactive authentication required.

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'systemctl|poweroff|polkit|CAP_SYS_BOOT|sudoers' src/pi/ deploy/ -- map current shutdown auth path
Root cause documented in fix commit message: which auth path was chosen (A/B/C) + WHY (security + footprint + durability)
Synthetic regression: mock subprocess.run to /sbin/systemctl poweroff returns exit 0 with the fix applied; FAILS pre-fix in equivalent unprivileged context
Deploy integration: deploy/deploy-pi.sh installs the polkit rule (or equivalent unit-file change) idempotently
Real-world drain 23 gate: prior boot journal contains 'Reached target Shutdown' or 'Powering off' line (Spool verifies via grep)
invariants: battery_health_log close-out still writes runtime_seconds + end_timestamp BEFORE poweroff invocation (analytics integrity)
V0.24.1 ladder WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER state-machine progression preserved (orthogonal)
No new privilege escalation beyond what is needed for poweroff specifically

stories.title: I-037 startup_log canary false-positive -- fix US-330 race-guard
stories.intent: PLACEHOLDER. V0.27.7 US-330 race-guard (3x retry around journalctl --list-boots for SD-card I/O contention) introduced a regression: startup_log.prior_boot_clean returns 1 unconditionally since 2026-05-12. Hypothesis (pending Ralph diagnosis): retry-fallback path returns default 1 on timeout OR shutdown-signature regex was loosened in US-330 patch. Fix: prior_boot_clean=0 (or NULL) when no shutdown signature found in prior-boot journal; 1 ONLY on recognized signature match. Preserve US-330's race-guard intent (no false-NEGATIVE under SD-I/O contention) without introducing false-POSITIVE on hard-crash.

filesToTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- audit _readBootList retry path + shutdown-signature matcher; fix returns 0/NULL on no-match instead of default 1)
tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_canary.py (NEW PLACEHOLDER -- synthetic regression: hard-crash + graceful both produce correct canary value)
doNotTouch: US-330's retry-count + retry-backoff parameters (the race-guard intent is correct; only the timeout-fallback default is wrong)
TD-051 orphan-cleanup unit-ordering -- separate cleaner-fix candidate; not in V0.27.11 scope unless US-330 retry path proves unfixable

groundingRefs.value: Pre-V0.27.7 (2026-05-08/09) prior_boot_clean values: 0, 0, 0 (honest). Post-V0.27.7 (2026-05-12 onward) prior_boot_clean values: 1, 1, 1, ..., 1 (every record, despite hard-crashes).

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'prior_boot_clean|_readBootList|shutdown.*signature|Reached target Shutdown' src/pi/ -- map canary code path
Root cause documented in fix commit message: which exact line/branch in US-330 (or related) yielded the unconditional 1
Synthetic regression PASS-FAIL pair: hard-crash scenario (kill -9 service + reboot) -> prior_boot_clean=0; graceful-poweroff scenario -> prior_boot_clean=1
US-330 race-guard intent preserved: journalctl --list-boots timeout retry still defends against false-NEGATIVE under SD-I/O contention
Real-world drain 23 gate: canary credibly distinguishes I-036-fixed (1) vs I-036-still-broken (0)
invariants: US-330 race-guard retry count + backoff preserved (no regression on SD-I/O-contention false-negative)
startup_log writer call-site contract preserved (no schema or column rename)
boot_reason.py public API surface preserved (only internal heuristic changes)
